simpleMode.confirmEnable:
Switch to Simple Mode?

Simple Mode hides most ECS component panels (attributes, skills, moves, items, equipment, relationships, custom components, etc.) and uses StatusEffect as the core state mechanism.

Suitable for lightweight games or beginners. The page will refresh after switching.
simpleMode.confirmDisable:
Switch to Full Mode?

Full Mode displays all ECS component panels (attributes, skills, moves, items, equipment, relationships, custom components, etc.) with complete state management capabilities.

Suitable for complex game mechanics. The page will refresh after switching.

apiConfig.title: API Configuration
apiConfig.importSuccess: Configuration imported successfully! Please review and save.
apiConfig.sharedConfigDesc: All models use the same settings
apiConfig.reasoningHint: Reasoning options apply to models like o1, o3 that support reasoning
apiConfig.recRetrievalDesc: Retrieval & Update models need low latency (high prefill speed), fast throughput, and minimal content moderation. The models below can reach ~200 token/s and complete retrieval in about 3–6s. Best value: google/gemini-2.5-flash-lite.
apiConfig.recRetrievalNote: Some Qwen and GPT models can also work, but typically have stricter content moderation — try at your own discretion.
apiConfig.recGenerationDesc: Generation models need good instruction-following for structured output. The options below are solid choices; premium models (purple) offer higher quality if budget allows.
apiConfig.recGenerationNote: Smaller models like x-ai/grok-4.1-fast or google/gemini-3-flash-preview can work but produce noticeably lower quality text.
apiConfig.recFootnote: ⚠ All three model slots require Structured Output support. Click a model name to auto-fill.

others.disableDocHint: When disabled, this document will never be recalled
others.staticPriorityHint: When enabled, this document bypasses RAG search and is inserted directly by priority. RAG results default to priority 50; higher values appear first
others.conditionHint:
Set natural language conditions for when LLM should recall this document. Examples:
• State-based: 'when character is in combat'
• Task-based: 'when describing magic battles'

If not set, LLM will flexibly decide based on current task.
